§6Syngas§f

§aSynthesis gas from coal and biomass — a versatile fuel and chemical feedstock!§f

Syngas (synthesis gas) is a flammable, combustible gas produced in the §bPyrolysis Oven§f from coal, biomass, or charcoal. With §e1.65 MTU§f and §e2.06 MHE§f per bucket, it serves primarily as an energy-dense gaseous fuel.

§6Production§f:
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f:
  §b–§f Coal + §e500mB§f Steam → §e1,000mB§f Syngas.
  §b–§f Coal Powder + §e500mB§f Steam → §e1,000mB§f Syngas.
  §b–§f Coal Coke + §e250mB§f Steam → §e1,000mB§f Syngas (most efficient).
  §b–§f §e4×§f Biomass → §e1,000mB§f Syngas.
  §b–§f Charcoal → §e1,000mB§f Syngas.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e260mB§f Syngas → §e1×§f Solid Fuel (more efficient than solidification).
§b• Industrial Solidification§f: §e650mB§f Syngas → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Fuel§f: Burn in generators for §e1.65 MTU§f / §e2.06 MHE§f per bucket.

§eTip§f: Syngas is not part of the oil processing chain per se, but is an excellent supplement to your power grid. Coal Coke + Steam gives the most efficient conversion. Pipe syngas directly into a §bFluid Burner§f for a self-heating setup!
